title: lonicera praeflorens var. japonica
intro: Lonicera praeflorens var. japonica, also known as the Japanese honeysuckle or Wood's honeysuckle, is a type of flowering vine in the Honeysuckle family, native to Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Lonicera praeflorens var. japonica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and gastrointestinal issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
